mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 0.66% signals accommodative stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through improved earnings visibility.
evidence_refs: fact:current PAYEMS at 145,798 thousand suggests near-threshold employment levels
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained job creation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where PAYEMS monthly growth exceeds 150,000, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real retail sales (RSAFS) combined with a rising unemployment rate signals weakening consumer demand and deteriorating labor market conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month decline > 0.5% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:current UNRATE at 4.7% and RSAFS at 473,991 million
regime_hint: early-stage demand deterioration with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or joint interaction effect shows zero correlation with forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 105.0 (index 2017=100) signals robust manufacturing and supply-side capacity utilization, supporting corporate profitability and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through improved operational efficiency.
evidence_refs: fact:current INDPRO at 104.5548 is near threshold
regime_hint: supply-side expansion with capacity utilization gains
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns when INDPRO > 105.0 are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 1,200 thousand units signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as construction and related sectors contract.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1,200 thousand units for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:current HOUST at 1,246 thousand units is above threshold
regime_hint: demand-side contraction with housing market deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where HOUST < 1,200 for 2+ consecutive months, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated federal funds rate above 2.0% signals monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as real yields increase.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 0.66% and CPIAUCSL at 244.456
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation persistence
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the joint interaction effect shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year window).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline due to lower consumer spending and potential recession signals.
evidence_refs: fact:current UNRATE at 4.7% is below threshold
regime_hint: labor market deterioration with recession risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with rising retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust aggregate demand and economic expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings and economic momentum.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 2.0% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1 at 16,804.1 billion and RSAFS at 473,991 million
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained demand growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the joint interaction effect shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window).
